prompts.content: A photograph with someone cut out of it.
A border that cuts through a cemetery.
A song everyone knows but nobody remembers learning.
A door that opens onto nothing.
A monument nobody can explain anymore.
A recipe with one ingredient unspecified.
A building that kept the name of what it used to be.
A law that never got repealed.
A word that changed meaning completely.
A map of a place that no longer exists.
A photograph taken seconds before.
A coin that is no longer legal tender.
A flag from a country that no longer exists.
A letter that was never sent.
A key to a door that no longer exists.
A road that ends abruptly.
A path worn through grass that nobody planned.
A treaty that was never ratified.

{smell}
{concept}

smell: a room that belonged to someone who has died
candle smoke after it's blown out
food from a country you've left
an old book with handwriting in the margins
a church in a country you don't know
the inside of an old car

prompts.content:
{emotion}
{object}

emotion: the embarrassment of caring too much
pride in something you had no part in
recognising someone you loved in a stranger's gesture
the guilt of forgetting a face
being moved by something you thought was beneath you
the feeling that a chapter has ended but nobody said so
the specific sadness of photographs of strangers
homesickness for somewhere you've never been
object: a map of a place that no longer exists
a flag from a country that no longer exists
a recipe with one ingredient unspecified
a monument nobody can explain anymore
a song everyone knows but nobody remembers learning
a door that opens onto nothing
a law that never got repealed
a word that changed meaning completely
a border that cuts through a cemetery
a letter that was never sent

prompts.content:
{color}
{concept}

color: the blue of a bruise on its third day

prompts.content: What's the last thing to disappear from a language when a community stops speaking it?
Two people share a memory of the same event. What are they each wrong about?
What gets lost when something becomes official?
Think of an institution that outlived its reason. What keeps it going?
What's the difference between a tradition and a habit?
When did something you used to believe become something you used to believe?
What would you have to forget to be a different person?
Find the moment where something became irreversible.
Find the accident that became permanent.
What has to be forgotten for this to seem normal?
